summary: Completed security vulnerability testing for ADSSIMSIM ad campaign platform. Identified CRITICAL XSS vulnerabilities in company signup form and multiple input validation issues. Frontend validation is insufficient and relies entirely on backend/Supabase RLS policies.

backend_issues.note: Backend testing not performed as this is a Supabase-only app. All backend logic is in Supabase edge functions and RLS policies which cannot be directly tested from frontend.

critical_vulnerabilities.component: Company Signup Form - Company Name Field
critical_vulnerabilities.issue: No input sanitization or XSS protection on company name field
critical_vulnerabilities.evidence: All XSS payloads accepted: , , javascript:alert('XSS'),
critical_vulnerabilities.impact: Attackers can inject malicious JavaScript that executes when company name is displayed anywhere in the app (brand listings, campaign pages, etc.)
critical_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement DOMPurify or similar sanitization library. Add Content Security Policy headers. Validate and sanitize all user input on both frontend and backend.

critical_vulnerabilities.issue: Application can be embedded in iframes - vulnerable to clickjacking attacks
critical_vulnerabilities.evidence: Successfully embedded page in iframe without restrictions
critical_vulnerabilities.impact: Attackers can overlay the application in an invisible iframe and trick users into performing unintended actions
affected_files: All pages - requires server-side header configuration
critical_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Add X-Frame-Options: DENY or SAMEORIGIN header. Alternatively, use CSP frame-ancestors directive.

critical_vulnerabilities.issue: Email field accepts SQL injection patterns and invalid email formats
critical_vulnerabilities.evidence: Accepted: admin'--, admin' OR '1'='1, '; DROP TABLE companies;--
critical_vulnerabilities.impact: While Supabase may prevent actual SQL injection, accepting invalid emails can lead to authentication issues and potential security bypasses
critical_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Add proper email format validation using regex or validation library (Zod). Reject invalid email formats on frontend before submission.

critical_vulnerabilities.issue: Frontend validation may not prevent: negative cash_allotment, extremely large values (>1M), invalid date ranges, javascript: protocol in URLs
critical_vulnerabilities.evidence: Validation schema exists in campaignValidation.ts but XSS testing shows frontend may not sanitize all inputs
critical_vulnerabilities.impact: Attackers could attempt to create campaigns with malicious data. Actual impact depends on Supabase RLS policy enforcement.
critical_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Ensure Zod validation schema is applied to ALL form inputs. Add sanitization for text fields. Validate numeric ranges and date logic on frontend.

ui_bugs.issue: Form requires logo and banner uploads but doesn't provide clear guidance on image requirements

security_best_practices_missing.recommendation: Implement CSP headers to prevent XSS attacks
Add X-Frame-Options: DENY or SAMEORIGIN header to prevent clickjacking
Add DOMPurify to sanitize all user-generated content before rendering

security_best_practices_missing.issue: Frontend relies entirely on backend validation
security_best_practices_missing.recommendation: Implement defense-in-depth: validate on both frontend and backend

passed_tests: Offers page is accessible to unauthenticated users
Brand dashboard requires authentication (redirects to login)
Campaign validation schema exists and defines proper rules (Zod)
Form requires all mandatory fields before submission
No sensitive information exposed in page source (API keys, secrets)
Browser console shows no critical errors on main pages
No sensitive data stored in localStorage
Service Worker support available for PWA functionality

critical_priority: FIX CRITICAL XSS VULNERABILITY: Implement input sanitization for company name field using DOMPurify or similar library
Add email format validation to company signup form
Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to prevent XSS attacks
Add DOMPurify library to sanitize ALL user-generated content before rendering
Add X-Frame-Options header to prevent clickjacking attacks
high_priority: Review and ensure Zod validation schema is applied to ALL form inputs in CampaignCreationForm
Add frontend validation for campaign fields (cash_allotment range, date validation, URL protocol validation)
Test if XSS payloads can be stored in database and rendered on other pages (requires completing signup flow)
Implement defense-in-depth: validate on both frontend and backend, don't rely solely on Supabase RLS
medium_priority: Add input sanitization to campaign title and description fields
Validate destination_url to prevent javascript: and data: protocols
Add rate limiting for signup and campaign creation
Improve error messages to not leak system information
testing_notes: Could not complete full campaign creation flow due to required image uploads in signup
XSS testing shows frontend accepts malicious input - actual storage/rendering needs verification
Supabase RLS policies may provide backend protection but frontend should also validate
Recommend creating test brand account manually to test full campaign creation flow

additional_notes.architecture: Supabase-backed React frontend. No traditional backend server. All backend logic in Supabase edge functions and RLS policies.
additional_notes.testing_limitations: Frontend-only testing as requested. Cannot directly test Supabase RLS policies or edge functions. Actual security depends heavily on backend implementation.
additional_notes.critical_finding: XSS vulnerability in company signup form is CRITICAL and should be fixed immediately before production deployment.
additional_notes.recommendation: Perform penetration testing with a complete brand account to test full campaign creation, editing, and deletion flows.
